title: Scaffold project and implement domain entities + SOLID source contracts
description:
## Goal
Set up the Flutter project structure and implement the foundational domain layer (entities, value objects, source contracts, repository interface, exchange capability registry) following SOLID principles and TDD.

## Effort Estimate
8 hours (one focused implementation slice).

## Implementation
1. Scaffold Flutter project
   - Create lib/ folder structure: core/, domain/, data/, presentation/, ui/.
   - Add pubspec.yaml dependencies: flutter_riverpod, riverpod, http, web_socket_channel, candlesticks, fl_chart, drift, sqlite3_flutter_libs, local_auth, flutter_secure_storage, go_router, freezed_annotation, json_annotation.
   - Add dev dependencies: build_runner, freezed, json_serializable, drift_dev, flutter_lints, mocktail.

2. Define domain value objects and entities (TDD)
   - lib/domain/entities/symbol.dart: base/quote assets, normalised id, exchange-specific rawSymbol.
   - lib/domain/entities/venue.dart: enum or sealed class for binance, bybit, okx, coinbase.
   - lib/domain/entities/ticker.dart: lastPrice, bid, ask, change24h, change24hPercent, volume, timestamp.
   - lib/domain/entities/candle.dart: OHLCV + timestamp.
   - lib/domain/entities/order_book.dart: bids/asks lists + spread.
   - lib/domain/entities/trade.dart: price, amount, side, time.
   - Use freezed/immutable value objects with equality and toString.

3. Define SOLID source contracts
   - lib/domain/sources/ticker_source.dart: Future<Result<Ticker>> fetchTicker(Symbol s).
   - lib/domain/sources/candle_source.dart: Future<Result<List<Candle>>> fetchCandles(Symbol s, Timeframe tf, {int? limit}).
   - lib/domain/sources/order_book_source.dart: Future<Result<OrderBook>> fetchOrderBook(Symbol s, {int? depth}).
   - lib/domain/sources/trade_source.dart: Future<Result<List<Trade>>> fetchTrades(Symbol s, {int? limit}).
   - lib/domain/sources/market_stream_source.dart: Stream<Result<TickerUpdate>>, Stream<Result<OrderBookUpdate>>, Stream<Result<Trade>> watch... methods.

4. Define repository interface and registry
   - lib/domain/repositories/market_data_repository.dart: abstract methods returning Result for sync operations and Result streams for live data.
   - lib/domain/registry/exchange_capability.dart: which features each venue supports (REST ticker, WS order book, etc.).

5. Implement core result type and failures
   - lib/core/result.dart: sealed Result<T> with Success and Failure; map, flatMap, fold helpers.
   - lib/core/failures.dart: NetworkFailure, ParseFailure, UnsupportedFeatureFailure, UnknownFailure.

6. Unit tests
   - test/domain/entities/symbol_test.dart
     - test_symbol_normalization_lowercase_input_converted_to_uppercase
     - test_symbol_invalid_characters_rejected
   - test/domain/entities/ticker_test.dart
     - test_ticker_spread_computed_as_ask_minus_bid
     - test_ticker_negative_price_rejected_or_documented
   - test/core/result_test.dart
     - test_result_map_transforms_success_value
     - test_result_map_preserves_failure
     - test_result_flatMap_short_circuits_on_failure
     - test_result_fold_returns_left_on_failure

## Success Criteria
- [ ] flutter pub get succeeds and all dependencies resolve.
- [ ] lib/ folder structure follows SOLID layers with no files in wrong layer.
- [ ] All domain entities are immutable freezed classes with equality.
- [ ] All source contracts return Result<T> and streams return Result<T> so failures are explicit.
- [ ] Result and Failure types compile and all 6+ unit tests pass.
- [ ] flutter analyze returns zero issues.
- [ ] flutter format --set-exit-if-changed lib test exits 0.
- [ ] No UI, REST, WebSocket, or exchange-specific code exists yet.

## Key Considerations
**Symbol normalization edge cases:**
- Inputs may be mixed case (btc/usdt) or already upper case (BTC/USDT); normalize to uppercase internally.
- Reject symbols containing whitespace or empty strings; use const assertion in tests.
- Preserve exchange-specific raw symbol (e.g. BTC-USDT for Coinbase, BTCUSDT for Binance) without leaking it into generic logic.

**Value-object equality:**
- Two Tickers with identical field values must be equal; two with different timestamps must not be equal.
- Equality on doubles must handle small precision differences via fixed-decimal types if available, otherwise document that exact-bit equality is required.

**Result type design:**
- Prefer explicit Result over exceptions for expected failures (network, parse, unsupported feature).
- UnknownFailure is for truly unexpected errors and must carry stack trace or original error.

**Freezed + code generation:**
- Run build_runner once to verify code generation works; commit generated files if team convention requires.
- If freezed causes analyzer issues, ensure build_runner output is included in analysis.

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ No mutable entity classes (domain objects must be immutable).
- ❌ No throwing exceptions from source contracts (use Result).
- ❌ No UI or exchange-specific code in domain layer.
- ❌ No TODO stubs without a linked br issue number.
- ❌ No tautological tests that only verify constructors or field existence.
close_reason: Completed: scaffolded project, implemented domain entities, Result type, failures, source contracts, repository interface, and exchange capability registry. All tests, analyze, and format pass.

title: Implement local biometric/PIN auth guard
design:
## Goal
Implement a local biometric/PIN authentication guard that blocks access to the app's main content until the user authenticates. The guard must work offline, use the platform's biometric APIs through the local_auth plugin, and fall back to a PIN when biometrics are unavailable or fail.

## Effort Estimate
6–8 hours

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `flutter analyze` returns no errors or warnings.
- [ ] `flutter test` passes all new and existing tests.
- [ ] `dart format --set-exit-if-changed lib test` exits cleanly.
- [ ] `LocalAuthService` interface is abstract and lives in `lib/domain/auth/`; implementation lives in `lib/data/auth/` and depends only on `local_auth`.
- [ ] `AuthNotifier` exposes a sealed/enum `AuthState` and transitions through `unknown`, `authenticated`, `unauthenticated`, and `error` states.
- [ ] `AuthGateScreen` blocks the main app content until `AuthState.authenticated` is emitted; shows a biometric button, PIN fallback input, and human-readable error messages.
- [ ] `lib/main.dart` wraps the app so the gate is the first screen shown.
- [ ] Tests verify notifier state transitions for: biometric available/success, biometric failure, biometric cancellation, PIN success, PIN failure, and sign-out.
- [ ] Widget test verifies gate shows blocked content before auth and replaces with app content after successful auth.
- [ ] No API secrets, PINs, or biometric data are persisted; only a runtime auth flag is held in notifier state.

## Implementation Checklist

### Domain layer (`lib/domain/auth/`)
- [ ] Create `lib/domain/auth/auth_failure.dart` with failure subtypes:
  - `BiometricNotAvailableFailure`
  - `AuthCancelledFailure`
  - `AuthFailedFailure`
  - `PinNotSetFailure`
  - `PinMismatchFailure`
- [ ] Create `lib/domain/auth/local_auth_service.dart` abstract interface:
  - `Future<Result<bool>> authenticate()`
  - `Future<bool> canCheckBiometrics()`

### Data layer (`lib/data/auth/`)
- [ ] Create `lib/data/auth/local_auth_service_impl.dart` implementing `LocalAuthService`.
- [ ] Use `local_auth` `LocalAuthentication`.
- [ ] On success return `Success(true)`.
- [ ] Map plugin exceptions/cancellations to appropriate `AuthFailure`.

### Presentation layer (`lib/presentation/auth/`)
- [ ] Create `lib/presentation/auth/auth_state.dart` with `AuthState` enum/sealed class: `unknown`, `unauthenticated`, `authenticated`, `error(Failure)`.
- [ ] Create `lib/presentation/auth/auth_notifier.dart` extending `StateNotifier<AuthState>`.
- [ ] Methods: `checkBiometricAvailability()`, `authenticateWithBiometrics()`, `authenticateWithPin(String pin)`, `signOut()`.
- [ ] Create `lib/presentation/auth/auth_guard_provider.dart` exposing the notifier provider.

### UI layer (`lib/ui/auth/`)
- [ ] Create `lib/ui/auth/auth_gate_screen.dart` as full-screen gate.
- [ ] Watch auth state; show loading for `unknown`, auth controls for `unauthenticated`, app content for `authenticated`, and error message for `error`.
- [ ] Provide biometric button, PIN text field, submit button, and sign-out button when authenticated.

### Integration (`lib/main.dart`)
- [ ] Update `main.dart` to show `AuthGateScreen` as `home` or initial route, with the real app content as its child.

### Tests
- [ ] Create `test/presentation/auth/auth_notifier_test.dart` using mocktail for `LocalAuthService`.
- [ ] Create `test/ui/auth/auth_gate_screen_test.dart` using `ProviderScope` overrides.

## Key Considerations

**Edge Case: Biometric unavailable**
- `canCheckBiometrics()` returns false on simulators or unsupported devices.
- UI must still show PIN fallback; notifier does not emit an error just because biometrics are unavailable.

**Edge Case: Biometric cancelled by user**
- Plugin may return false or throw `PlatformException` for cancellation.
- Map to `AuthCancelledFailure` and emit `AuthState.error` without locking the user out permanently.

**Edge Case: PIN input**
- Empty PIN should be treated as mismatch, not crash.
- Hard-code a demo PIN constant (e.g., `"1234"`) for this iteration; do not store it in secure storage or persist it.
- Future iteration can replace with secure storage-backed PIN.

**Edge Case: No initial state**
- `AuthState.unknown` should show a loading indicator; do not auto-trigger biometric on first build to avoid permission dialogs during widget tests.
- Notifier constructor starts at `unknown`; caller explicitly invokes `checkBiometricAvailability()` or authentication.

**Edge Case: Sign out**
- `signOut()` returns state to `unauthenticated`; gate reappears.

**Concurrency**
- All async methods should be awaited; no parallel auth requests should corrupt state. StateNotifier's synchronous state setter naturally serializes updates.

**Security**
- Do not log the PIN.
- Do not persist auth state; it is runtime-only.
- Do not store actual PINs or secrets.

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ No exchange API secrets or private keys stored in the Flutter app.
- ❌ No direct exchange-specific logic in UI or use cases.
- ❌ No giant exchange interface that forces every venue to implement unsupported methods.
- ❌ No TODO stubs for core domain entities or repository contract.
- ❌ No polling for real-time data; use WebSocket streams for active symbols.
- ❌ No ad-hoc state mutation outside notifiers.
- ❌ No deviation from the mockup design system without explicit user approval.
- ❌ No `unwrap`/`expect`-style assumptions on plugin results.
- ❌ No hardcoded secrets beyond a single demo PIN constant.
- ❌ No testing of real biometric APIs; mock `LocalAuthService` in unit/widget tests.

## Verification Commands
```bash
export PATH=\"\$PATH:/Users/deepol/development/flutter/bin\"
flutter analyze
flutter test
dart format --set-exit-if-changed lib test
```
close_reason: Completed local biometric/PIN auth guard with notifier, service, and UI gate

title: Build Exchange Management screen for read-only API key input
description:
## Goal
Implement the Exchange Management screen where users can add, edit, and remove read-only exchange API keys for Binance, Bybit, OKX, and Coinbase. Keys are stored securely; only read-only public endpoints are exercised for validation.

## Implementation
1. Domain additions
   - Add  value object: venue, apiKey, secret, password, isEnabled, createdAt.
   - Add  interface: save, load, delete, validate, list enabled venues.
   - Add failure types: InvalidCredentialsFailure, SecureStorageFailure.

2. Secure storage data source
   - lib/data/datasources/local/secure_credentials_store.dart
   - Use  with key namespaced by venue id.
   - Never log values; write tests with a mock store.

3. Validation use case
   - lib/domain/usecases/validate_exchange_credentials.dart
   - Call private read-only balance endpoint for the venue using the provided credentials.
   - Return Result<bool>; do not persist balance data.

4. Presentation
   - Add  and .
   - Add  for test-connection state.

5. UI
   - lib/ui/screens/exchange_management_screen.dart: list of venues with status.
   - lib/ui/screens/exchange_detail_edit_screen.dart or modal: API key/secret inputs, enabled toggle, test connection, delete.
   - Use obscured text fields with reveal toggle.
   - Show permission hint: read-only keys recommended.

6. Tests
   - test/domain/usecases/validate_exchange_credentials_test.dart
   - test/data/datasources/local/secure_credentials_store_test.dart (mocked)
   - test/presentation/exchange_credentials_notifier_test.dart
   - widget test for ExchangeManagementScreen navigation and form.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] User can enable/disable each venue and save preference.
- [ ] API key and secret are stored in secure storage and never logged.
- [ ] Test connection calls a read-only endpoint and reports success/failure.
- [ ] Invalid/empty credentials produce a clear error without crashing.
- [ ] Delete removes keys and disables venue.
- [ ] All new code follows SOLID layers and passes flutter analyze/format/test.

## Key Considerations
**Security:**
- Keys must be trimmed but never exposed in logs or UI beyond reveal toggle.
- Warn if user enters keys with trading/withdrawal permissions (detect via exchange permissions endpoint where available).
- Secure storage migration handled by  v10 cipher defaults.

**Edge cases:**
- No secure storage available (emulator/simulator) → graceful error message.
- User cancels biometric during reveal → keep field obscured.
- Test connection fails due to network → distinguish from invalid key.
- Multiple venues saved → list loads asynchronously per venue.

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ No storing keys in SharedPreferences or plain text.
- ❌ No sending keys to analytics or crash reporting.
- ❌ No writing real credentials in tests.
- ❌ No full trading permissions required or assumed.
- ❌ No TODO stubs without linked br issue.

See also:
- [docs/screens.md](../docs/screens.md) for screen flows and states.
- [docs/architecture.md](../docs/architecture.md) for overall architecture.
- [AGENTS.md](../AGENTS.md) for project conventions and XP/SOLID rules.
close_reason: Completed Exchange Management screen with secure credential storage and read-only validation

title: Build Markets/Screener screen from mockups
design:
## Goal
Build the Markets/Screener screen matching the YouTrade mockup: a searchable, filterable list of markets showing symbol, name, venue, sparkline, last price, and 24h change.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `lib/ui/screens/markets_screen.dart` renders as a standalone Scaffold using the existing theme system.
- [ ] Screen shows a search bar, horizontal filter chips, header row (Symbol / Last · 24h), and a scrollable list of market tiles.
- [ ] Each market tile displays symbol, asset class tag, full name, venue, sparkline chart (fl_chart), last price, and 24h change percentage with bullish/bearish coloring.
- [ ] Tapping a filter chip visually selects it and filters the visible market list.
- [ ] Typing in the search field filters the list by symbol, name, or venue.
- [ ] `test/ui/screens/markets_screen_test.dart` verifies no overflow, renders search/filter area, and renders market list tiles.
- [ ] `flutter test`, `flutter analyze`, and `dart format --set-exit-if-changed lib test` pass.

## Implementation Checklist
- [ ] Create `lib/presentation/providers/market_screener_provider.dart` with static mock market data, search query state, and selected filter state.
- [ ] Create `lib/ui/widgets/markets/filter_chips.dart` for the horizontal filter chip list.
- [ ] Create `lib/ui/widgets/markets/sparkline_chart.dart` wrapping `fl_chart` LineChart for sparklines.
- [ ] Create `lib/ui/widgets/markets/market_list_tile.dart` for individual market rows.
- [ ] Create `lib/ui/screens/markets_screen.dart` composing the above widgets.
- [ ] Create `test/ui/screens/markets_screen_test.dart` with widget tests using `ProviderScope`.
- [ ] Run verification commands and fix issues.

## Key Considerations

**Empty / no-match states**
- When search or filter yields zero markets, show a centered "No markets found" message instead of an empty list.

**Overflow safety**
- Use `Expanded`/`Flexible` for the market list inside a `Column` so the screen does not overflow on small devices.
- Sparkline width is fixed (46px) and the price column has a fixed width (78px); remaining space flexes for name/venue.

**Mock data only**
- This screen uses static mock data; no real exchange REST/WebSocket calls are made in the UI.

**Filtering semantics**
- "All" shows every mock market.
- Other chips filter by asset class: Crypto, Forex, Equities, Commodities.
- Search matches symbol, name, or venue display name case-insensitively.

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ No exchange API secrets or private keys in the Flutter app.
- ❌ No direct exchange-specific logic in UI or use cases; mock data is injected via provider.
- ❌ No TODO stubs for core UI widgets.
- ❌ No polling for real-time data in this screen (static mock data).
- ❌ No ad-hoc state mutation outside notifiers/providers.
- ❌ No deviation from mockup design system without explicit user approval.
close_reason: Completed Markets/Screener screen matching mockups with widget tests

title: Test Quality Improvement: YouTrade Flutter
description:
## Goal
Improve the YouTrade Flutter test suite's real effectiveness by removing false-confidence tests, strengthening weak assertions, and adding missing corner-case coverage.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All tautological/mock-only/evergreen RED tests removed or replaced
- [ ] All YELLOW tests strengthened with exact-value or behavior assertions
- [ ] P0 modules (auth, exchange credentials, market data parsing) have corner-case coverage
- [ ] Test suite still passes
- [ ] Coverage may decrease; quality must increase

## Scope
- test/core/result_test.dart
- test/domain/entities/*_test.dart
- test/data/datasources/**/*_test.dart
- test/data/repositories/*_test.dart
- test/presentation/**/*_test.dart
- test/ui/**/*_test.dart
- test/widget_test.dart
- integration_test/*_test.dart

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ Keeping tests "just in case"
- ❌ Replacing weak assertions with equally weak ones
- ❌ Adding coverage-only tests
- ❌ Testing mock behavior instead of production behavior

title: Remove tautological and false-confidence tests
description:
## Goal
Delete tests that provide false confidence by passing regardless of production correctness.

## Tests to Remove/Replace
- test/ui/screens/settings_screen_test.dart:139-151 - "biometric tile triggers availability check" only verifies mock method call, not user-visible behavior.
- test/widget_test.dart:11-24 - "App launches and shows auth gate" is a duplicate smoke test already covered by integration/app_test.dart and auth_gate_screen_test.dart.
- test/data/repositories/market_data_repository_impl_test.dart:45-86 - Weak assertions that only exercise MockMarketDataStore internals; replace with real source injection tests or remove.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Listed RED tests removed or replaced with behavior-verifying tests
- [ ] Test suite still passes
- [ ] No new tautological tests introduced

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ Keeping tests "just in case"
- ❌ Replacing with equally meaningless tests
- ❌ Adding coverage-only tests to compensate

## Tests to Remove/Replace
- test/ui/screens/settings_screen_test.dart:139-151 - "biometric tile triggers availability check" only verifies mock method call, not user-visible behavior. Replace with test that asserts the biometric availability dialog/gate is shown or hidden based on the service result.
- test/widget_test.dart:11-24 - "App launches and shows auth gate" is a duplicate smoke test already covered by integration/app_test.dart and auth_gate_screen_test.dart. Remove.

- [ ] Test suite still passes
- [ ] No new tautological tests introduced
- [ ] Coverage decrease is acceptable and documented

## Key Considerations
- When removing tests, ensure the behavior is still covered elsewhere (integration test or widget test).
- If replacing repository tests, inject deterministic fake VenueSources and assert exact returned values, not mock-store behavior.
- Run `flutter test` after each removal/replacement.

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ Keeping tests "just in case"
- ❌ Replacing with equally meaningless tests
- ❌ Adding coverage-only tests to compensate
close_reason: Completed: removed duplicate widget_test.dart, replaced mock-only sign-out and biometric tests with behavior-verifying state assertions, all tests pass.

title: Strengthen weak assertions in data source and provider tests
description:
## Goal
Replace weak assertions with exact-value checks that catch real production bugs.

## Tests to Strengthen
- test/data/datasources/local/market_cache_data_source_test.dart:69-72 - "saves and retrieves candles" uses containsAll on close prices only; verify all OHLCV fields and ordering.
- test/data/datasources/local/market_cache_data_source_test.dart:74-86 - "saves and retrieves order book" checks isNotNull + first prices; verify all bid/ask levels, amounts, and timestamp roundtrip.
- test/data/datasources/remote/binance/binance_rest_client_test.dart:43-49 - "fetchTicker returns Failure on non-200" only checks isA<Err>(); assert exact NetworkFailure message.
- test/data/datasources/remote/bybit/bybit_rest_client_test.dart:47-53 - same non-200 weak assertion issue.
- test/data/repositories/market_data_repository_impl_test.dart:45-86 - all assertions (greaterThan(0), length) are too weak; inject deterministic fake sources and assert exact returned values.
- test/presentation/providers/market_data_providers_test.dart:139-275 - all AsyncData/AsyncError checks are type-only; assert exact emitted values and exact error types.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All weak assertions replaced with exact value or exact error checks
- [ ] Each test documents the specific bug it catches
- [ ] No regressions in existing passing tests

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ Replacing isA<Success>() with isA<AsyncData>() (still weak)
- ❌ Adding edge cases without meaningful assertions

## Implementation Checklist
- [ ] For each listed test, identify the specific production bug it should catch

- [ ] Add a comment above each strengthened test naming the bug it catches
- [ ] Run `flutter test` after each file update

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All weak assertions replaced with exact value or exact error checks
- [ ] Each test documents the specific bug it catches
- [ ] No regressions in existing passing tests
- [ ] `flutter test` passes

## Key Considerations
- Exact error assertions should verify the failure type AND message when the message carries diagnostic value.
- For repository tests, avoid relying on MockMarketDataStore randomness; seed Random or inject fakes.

- AsyncError assertions should verify the thrown Failure equals the repository's failure.

- ❌ Adding edge cases without meaningful assertions

title: Strengthen weak assertions in UI and notifier tests
description:
## Goal
Replace smoke-test assertions with behavior-verifying assertions in widget and notifier tests.

## Tests to Strengthen
- test/presentation/exchange/exchange_credentials_notifier_test.dart:94-115 - "save reloads credentials on success" only checks state type; assert exact credential list after reload.
- test/presentation/exchange/exchange_credentials_notifier_test.dart:137-158 - "delete reloads credentials on success" same issue.
- test/ui/screens/compare_screen_test.dart:34-45 - "tapping a time range chip updates the selected range" asserts find.text('3M') which already exists; assert chart series changed or selected range state.
- test/ui/screens/portfolio_screen_test.dart:69-77 - "toggles theme mode" only checks screen still exists; assert actual theme brightness changed.
- test/ui/screens/trading_terminal_screen_test.dart:126-153 - smoke tests only verify widgets exist; assert actual data-driven content (prices, order book levels) rendered.
- test/ui/screens/settings_screen_test.dart:125-137 - "tapping sign out does not throw" is too weak; assert auth state becomes unauthenticated.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All listed tests assert observable behavior, not just existence/type
- [ ] Each test would fail if the corresponding production behavior breaks
- [ ] No new weak assertions introduced

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ Verifying widget existence only
- ❌ Asserting text that is static regardless of state
- ❌ "does not throw" as the only assertion

## Key Considerations
- Coverage is a secondary metric; assertion quality matters more.
- Manual mutation review is necessary because Flutter mutation testing tooling is immature.
- Document each injected bug and which test killed it.
- If a mutant survives, create a new task to add a test that kills it rather than lowering the bar.
- Do not optimize coverage by adding weak assertions.

- ❌ Optimizing coverage percentage without improving assertions
- ❌ Adding tests that cannot fail on production changes
- ❌ Declaring validation complete without evidence

title: Implement OKX and Coinbase market data clients
Implement Riverpod providers for market data streams
design:
## Goal
Refactor MarketDataRepositoryImpl so it can route market-data requests to real Binance/Bybit REST/WebSocket clients when configured, while preserving the mock store as an offline/demo fallback. Then add Riverpod providers that expose ticker, candle, order-book, and trade streams as AsyncValue states, plus a repository provider that wires real sources and a selected-symbol provider defaulting to BTC/USDT @ Binance.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `MarketDataRepositoryImpl` accepts `Map<Venue, VenueSources>` without breaking existing constructor callers.
- [ ] When a venue has `VenueSources`, REST and WebSocket methods use the real source; otherwise they fall back to the mock store.
- [ ] Unsupported-feature checks still return `UnsupportedFeatureFailure` before any source/mock is invoked.
- [ ] `lib/presentation/providers/repository_provider.dart` constructs the repository with real Binance/Bybit REST + WebSocket clients and a static capability registry.
- [ ] `lib/presentation/providers/selected_symbol_provider.dart` defaults to BTC/USDT on Binance.
- [ ] `lib/presentation/providers/market_data_providers.dart` exposes `tickerStreamProvider`, `candlesProvider`, `orderBookStreamProvider`, and `tradesStreamProvider` using `family` with `TradingSymbol`.
- [ ] Providers convert `Result` from the repository into `AsyncData`/`AsyncError` (throw failures so Riverpod wraps them).
- [ ] `test/presentation/providers/market_data_providers_test.dart` verifies AsyncData after `Success` and AsyncError after `Failure` for each provider.
- [ ] `flutter test`, `flutter analyze`, and `dart format --set-exit-if-changed lib test` pass.

## Implementation Checklist
- [ ] Study `MarketDataRepository`, `MarketDataRepositoryImpl`, exchange source contracts, Binance/Bybit clients, and `ExchangeCapabilityRegistry`.
- [ ] Define `VenueSources` in `lib/data/repositories/market_data_repository_impl.dart` bundling `TickerSource`, `CandleSource`, `OrderBookSource`, `TradeSource`, and `MarketStreamSource`.
- [ ] Refactor `MarketDataRepositoryImpl` constructor to accept optional `Map<Venue, VenueSources> venueSources`.
- [ ] Update each repository method to prefer real source when present and capable, else mock store, else `UnsupportedFeatureFailure`.
- [ ] Create `lib/presentation/providers/repository_provider.dart` with:
  - A private static registry implementation of `ExchangeCapabilityRegistry` covering Binance and Bybit with all `MarketDataFeature` values.
  - A `marketDataRepositoryProvider` `Provider<MarketDataRepository>` that instantiates `BinanceRestClient`, `BinanceWebSocketClient`, `BybitRestClient`, `BybitWebSocketClient`, and `MockMarketDataStore`, wiring them into `VenueSources` for Binance and Bybit.
- [ ] Create `lib/presentation/providers/selected_symbol_provider.dart` with a `StateProvider<TradingSymbol>` defaulting to `TradingSymbol(base: 'BTC', quote: 'USDT', venue: Venue.binance, rawSymbol: 'BTCUSDT')`.
- [ ] Create `lib/presentation/providers/market_data_providers.dart` with:
  - `tickerStreamProvider` (`StreamProvider.family<Ticker, TradingSymbol>`) via `repository.watchTicker(...)`.
  - `candlesProvider` (`FutureProvider.family<List<Candle>, TradingSymbol>`) via `repository.getCandles(..., Timeframe.h1)`.
  - `orderBookStreamProvider` (`StreamProvider.family<OrderBook, TradingSymbol>`) via `repository.watchOrderBook(...)`.
  - `tradesStreamProvider` (`StreamProvider.family<List<Trade>, TradingSymbol>`) via `repository.watchTrades(...)`.
  - Map each `Result` with `fold(...)` to throw `Failure` on error so Riverpod emits `AsyncError`.
- [ ] Add `test/presentation/providers/market_data_providers_test.dart`:
  - Fake `MarketDataRepository` returning `Success` for all methods.
  - Fake `MarketDataRepository` returning `Failure` for all methods.
  - One test per provider asserting `AsyncData`/`AsyncError`.
- [ ] Run `flutter test`, `flutter analyze`, and `dart format --set-exit-if-changed lib test`; fix issues.
- [ ] Commit all changes with the specified message.
- [ ] Close `youtrade-8s9` with the specified reason.

## Key Considerations

### Edge Case: Unsupported Venue
If the registry returns no capability for a venue, or the capability does not support the requested feature, the repository must immediately return `UnsupportedFeatureFailure` without touching real or mock sources.

### Edge Case: Source Present but Capability Offline
If `venueSources` contains a venue but the registry says the feature is unsupported, the repository returns `UnsupportedFeatureFailure` rather than using the source. Capability is the gate.

### Edge Case: Real Source Returns Failure
When a real source returns `Err(...)`, propagate that failure directly (do not fall back to mock). The caller/provider decides how to surface it.

### Edge Case: Empty `venueSources`
With `venueSources` null or empty, every supported-capability request falls back to `MockMarketDataStore`. Existing repository tests must continue to pass unchanged.

### Edge Case: Provider Disposal
`StreamProvider.family` will cancel the underlying stream when the provider is no longer listened to. No manual subscription management is required because Riverpod owns the provider lifecycle.

### Concurrency
The repository is stateless; multiple providers can call it concurrently. Real REST clients each own their own `http.Client`. WebSocket streams are created per `watch*` call.

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ No exchange API secrets or private keys in the Flutter app.
- ❌ No exchange-specific parsing or URL building in providers or UI.
- ❌ No giant exchange interface; keep source contracts small and segregated.
- ❌ No TODO stubs for repository or provider implementations.
- ❌ No polling for real-time data; use existing WebSocket streams.
- ❌ No ad-hoc state mutation outside notifiers/providers.
- ❌ No `AsyncValue` exposed as `Result`; providers unwrap `Result`.
- ❌ No direct `http` or `WebSocketChannel` usage outside the data layer.

title: Build Options Chain screen from mockups
design:
## Goal
Build the Options Chain screen from the YouTrade mockups using static synthetic options data. The screen must match the design system (dark/light, Flux/Carbon themes), show an underlying symbol header, an expiry selector, column headers, and a scrollable strike grid with calls/puts displaying bid/ask mark, IV, delta, volume, and open interest. This is a mock/synthetic-only implementation; real options data feed is deferred per epic scope.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `lib/ui/screens/options_chain_screen.dart` exists as a standalone `Scaffold` using the existing `AppTheme`/`AppColorTheme` system.
- [ ] `lib/ui/widgets/options_chain/` contains at least `expiry_selector.dart`, `strike_row.dart`, and `greek_badge.dart`.
- [ ] Static mock options data is provided in-screen or via a private mock helper; no real API/WebSocket calls are made from the UI.
- [ ] The screen renders the underlying symbol (BTC), spot price, expiry pills, section headers (Calls / Strike / Puts), sub-headers (IV, Δ, Mark), and at least 9 strike rows with calls and puts side-by-side.
- [ ] The ATM strike is visually highlighted and labelled, matching the mockup.
- [ ] Widget test `test/ui/screens/options_chain_screen_test.dart` passes, verifying no overflow and the presence of the expiry selector and strike rows.
- [ ] `flutter test`, `flutter analyze`, and `dart format --set-exit-if-changed lib test` all pass.
- [ ] `lib/main.dart` and global routing are not modified.
- [ ] No exchange API secrets, no TODO stubs for domain entities, no polling, and no direct exchange logic in UI.

## Implementation Checklist
- [ ] Read `/mockups/YouTrade.dc.html` Options Chain section (lines 414-447) and the theme files (`lib/presentation/theme/app_theme.dart`, `theme_extensions.dart`).
- [ ] Create `lib/ui/screens/options_chain_screen.dart` as a `ConsumerStatefulWidget` or `ConsumerWidget` Scaffold with synthetic state (selected expiry index, mock rows).
- [ ] Create `lib/ui/widgets/options_chain/expiry_selector.dart` for horizontally scrollable expiry pills.
- [ ] Create `lib/ui/widgets/options_chain/strike_row.dart` for a single 7-column grid row (call IV, call Δ, call Mark, Strike, put Mark, put Δ, put IV).
- [ ] Create `lib/ui/widgets/options_chain/greek_badge.dart` reusable small label/badge widget (used for IV/Δ labels and the OPTIONS class chip).
- [ ] Create `lib/ui/widgets/options_chain/options_header.dart` (optional) encapsulating symbol + spot price header.
- [ ] Create `lib/ui/widgets/options_chain/chain_column_headers.dart` (optional) encapsulating Calls/Strike/Puts and IV/Δ/Mark headers.
- [ ] Generate synthetic options data deterministically inside the screen (no external dependencies): underlying BTC ~$68k, strikes from ATM-4 steps to ATM+4 steps in $2k increments, expiries 26 JUN, 25 JUL, 29 AUG, 26 SEP.
- [ ] Add `test/ui/screens/options_chain_screen_test.dart` with pump-and-settle tests:
  - `options_chain_screen_renders_without_overflow`: wraps screen in `MaterialApp` with a dark `AppTheme.flux` theme, pumps, verifies no overflow via `takeException` and finds expiry selector / strike rows.
  - `options_chain_screen_shows_expiry_selector_and_strike_rows`: finds expiry chips and strike row text.
- [ ] Run `flutter test`, `flutter analyze`, `dart format --set-exit-if-changed lib test` and fix issues.
- [ ] Commit with the provided message.
- [ ] Close task with `br close youtrade-i0f --reason \"Completed Options Chain screen with synthetic data and widget tests\"`.

## Key Considerations

**Synthetic Data Only**
- All option values (IV, delta, mark, volume, OI) must be hard-coded or produced by a private helper inside the screen file. No repository call, no provider, no WebSocket subscription.

**Theme Conformance**
- Use `theme.extension<AppColorTheme>()!` for bullish/bearish/accent/border colors.
- Use `theme.textTheme.labelSmall/labelMedium/labelLarge/bodySmall` for typography and add `fontFamily: 'Geist'` / `fontFamily: 'Space Grotesk'` where the mockup uses those families.
- Text styles must respect the current theme; do not hard-code dark-theme colors.

**Layout Safety**
- The strike grid is dense on a 390pt-wide phone. Use `LayoutBuilder` or `Expanded` in row columns and ensure text does not overflow. Keep `fontSize` <= 9.5 for grid numbers.
- Wrap expiry pills in `SingleChildScrollView` with `scrollDirection: Axis.horizontal`.
- Wrap the full screen in `SafeArea` + `SingleChildScrollView` to avoid bottom overflow.

**Edge Cases**
- Empty/mock row list: the screen should still render headers and an empty-state message if rows are empty (defensive).
- Long spot prices: format with grouping separators and no fractional cents for BTC.
- Theme toggles: widget test should pass in both light and dark if feasible; at minimum test the default dark theme.

**Widget Test Quality**
- Tests must assert real outcomes, not existence of the widget class itself.
- Verify the expiry selector text (e.g., '26 JUN') appears and at least one strike row text (e.g., '70,000') is present.
- Use `tester.pumpWidget(...)` with a real `MaterialApp` and the app theme.

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ No exchange API secrets or private keys stored in the Flutter app.
- ❌ No direct exchange-specific logic in UI or use cases; architecture must go through repository/interfaces (N/A here because data is synthetic).
- ❌ No giant exchange interface forcing unsupported methods.
- ❌ No TODO stubs for core domain entities or repository contracts.
- ❌ No polling for real-time data; this screen is synthetic-only.
- ❌ No ad-hoc state mutation outside notifiers; use `setState` inside the screen state object for the synthetic UI state.
- ❌ No deviation from the mockup design system without explicit user approval.
- ❌ No `lib/main.dart` or global routing changes.

title: Build Trading Terminal screen from mockups
design:
## Goal
Build the Trading Terminal screen from the YouTrade mockups as a standalone Scaffold. The screen displays a symbol header, candlestick chart with MA7/MA25 labels and volume, time-frame selector, lower tab bar (Trade/Book/Info/Signals), and tab-specific panels: trade ticket, order book with depth bars, fundamentals card, and technical-signals gauge with oscillator/MA/pivot tables.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `lib/ui/screens/trading_terminal_screen.dart` exists, compiles, and is a standalone `Scaffold`.
- [ ] All new widgets live under `lib/ui/widgets/trading_terminal/`.
- [ ] Screen consumes existing providers: `tickerStreamProvider`, `candlesProvider`, `orderBookStreamProvider`, `tradesStreamProvider`.
- [ ] Chart renders via the `candlesticks` package, maps domain `Candle` entities to package `Candle`s, shows volume bars, and displays MA7/MA25 labels.
- [ ] Lower tabs (Trade/Book/Info/Signals) switch panels without errors.
- [ ] Trade ticket includes buy/sell toggle, order-type chips, leverage slider, size-percent buttons, price display, and a submit CTA.
- [ ] Order-book panel renders asks and bids with depth-bar backgrounds and a spread line.
- [ ] Widget test `test/ui/screens/trading_terminal_screen_test.dart` renders the screen without overflow and finds chart area, trade ticket, and tabs.
- [ ] `flutter analyze` passes with no errors or warnings.
- [ ] `flutter test` passes.
- [ ] `dart format --set-exit-if-changed lib test` passes.

## Implementation Checklist
- [ ] Read `mockups/YouTrade.dc.html` terminal section and existing theme/providers.
- [ ] Create `lib/presentation/providers/trading_terminal_provider.dart` with immutable `TradingTerminalState` and `TradingTerminalNotifier` for tab, side, order type, leverage, size percent, and timeframe.
- [ ] Create `lib/ui/widgets/trading_terminal/symbol_header.dart` with symbol, class chip, name/venue, price, 24h change, and 4-column stat strip.
- [ ] Create `lib/ui/widgets/trading_terminal/time_frame_selector.dart` for timeframe chips.
- [ ] Create `lib/ui/widgets/trading_terminal/candlestick_chart.dart` that watches `candlesProvider`, converts domain candles newest-to-oldest, computes MA7/MA25, and renders `Candlesticks` with app-matching style.
- [ ] Create `lib/ui/widgets/trading_terminal/order_book_panel.dart` that watches `orderBookStreamProvider`, shows asks/bids with depth bars and spread.
- [ ] Create `lib/ui/widgets/trading_terminal/trade_ticket.dart` with side toggle, order-type chips, leverage slider, size-percent chips, order size/cost summary, and submit button.
- [ ] Create `lib/ui/widgets/trading_terminal/signal_gauge.dart` with SVG-style gauge, verdict, oscillator table, moving-average table, and pivot grid.
- [ ] Create `lib/ui/widgets/trading_terminal/fundamentals_card.dart` with tag cards and stat grid.
- [ ] Create `lib/ui/widgets/trading_terminal/lower_tabs.dart` for the tab bar.
- [ ] Implement `lib/ui/screens/trading_terminal_screen.dart` composing the above widgets in a scrollable layout.
- [ ] Add `test/ui/screens/trading_terminal_screen_test.dart` overriding `marketDataRepositoryProvider` with a deterministic fake repository.
- [ ] Run verification commands and fix any issues.

## Key Considerations

**Empty / Error States**
- `candlesProvider` error or empty list must show a loading/error placeholder, not a blank chart.
- Empty order book must display dashes for spread and zero-depth bars.

**Async Data**
- All market providers are asynchronous; use `AsyncValue` widgets and avoid blocking the UI.
- Widget test must override the repository so no real network/WebSocket calls are made.

**Overflow Avoidance**
- The terminal content is tall; wrap the body in a scrollable column and keep nested flex depths shallow.
- Use `LayoutBuilder` or `Expanded` carefully inside scrollable parents to avoid unbounded constraints.

**Leverage Bounds**

title: YouTrade Flutter app: multi-venue trading terminal
description:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Build a Flutter mobile trading terminal called YouTrade with the screens and visual design pixel-for-pixel faithful to /mockups/YouTrade.dc.html.
- Reproduce exactly these screens: Home / Aggregated Portfolio, Trading Terminal, Markets / Screener, Exchange Detail, Options Chain, Compare, Orders & History, Account / Settings.
- Preserve the visual identity: iOS 26 Liquid Glass-style device frame, dark-first palette, Flux (dir:b) and Carbon (dir:a) visual directions, Geist/Space Grotesk/JetBrains Mono typography.
- Reproduce every interactive component from the mockups: iOS status bar, app header with theme/direction toggles, bottom tab navigation, exchange allocation bar, exchange cards, position list, equity curve canvas, symbol chips, candlestick chart with MA7/MA25 and volume, time-frame selector, compare chart, order book with depth bars, trade ticket with buy/sell toggle / order-type chips / leverage slider / size-percent buttons, lower tabs (Trade/Book/Info/Signals), technical-signals gauge, oscillator/MA/pivot tables, fundamentals card, options chain grid, market screener with filters and sparklines, orders tabs (Open/History/Positions), settings list with connected exchanges and appearance toggles.
- Follow SOLID architecture: domain abstractions, per-exchange implementations, Riverpod state layer, dumb UI.
- Support mock-first offline data and real public market data online; no exchange API secrets in the mobile app for this epic.
- Integrate public REST + WebSocket market data from Binance, Bybit, OKX, and Coinbase via direct Flutter calls.
- Add local biometric/PIN gate before portfolio/trading screens; no cloud account required.
- Keep build under ten minutes: flutter test, flutter analyze, flutter format must pass.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All mockup screens exist and are navigable with the same layout, spacing, typography, and colors as /mockups/YouTrade.dc.html.
- [ ] Domain entities, source contracts, repository, and use cases are implemented and unit-tested.
- [ ] At least two exchanges stream real tickers/candles/order books/trades through a unified abstraction.
- [ ] Offline mode displays mock data with a visible Demo/Offline indicator.
- [ ] Theme matches mockups: dark first, Flux/Carbon toggle, iOS-first styling.
- [ ] Local auth gate blocks access until biometric/PIN succeeds.
- [ ] flutter test, flutter analyze, flutter format --set-exit-if-changed lib test pass.

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- No exchange API secrets or private keys stored in the Flutter app (security: first build is public data only).
- No direct exchange-specific logic in UI or use cases (architecture: must go through repository/interfaces).
- No giant exchange interface that forces every venue to implement unsupported methods (SOLID: interface segregation).
- No TODO stubs for core domain entities or repository contract (completeness: core abstraction must be real).
- No polling for real-time data; use WebSocket streams for active symbols (performance: respect rate limits).
- No ad-hoc state mutation outside notifiers (state management: emit new state).
- No deviation from the mockup design system without explicit user approval (design fidelity).

## Approach
Adopt layered/clean architecture driven by SOLID. Domain defines small contracts (TickerSource, CandleSource, OrderBookSource, TradeSource, MarketStreamSource) and a MarketDataRepository. Each exchange provides a REST client, WebSocket client, and adapter that transform exchange DTOs into domain entities. A registry maps venues to clients so new exchanges can be added without editing core logic. Riverpod providers expose AsyncValue/Stream states to dumb UI widgets. Drift caches market data; mock data store provides offline/demo fallback. UI is implemented screen-by-screen to match the mockups, with a shared theme extension and reusable widget kit.

## Child Epics
- youtrade-0z9: Phase 1 - Scaffold + domain entities + SOLID contracts
- youtrade-fl6: Phase 2 - Data layer (mock store, cache, exchange clients)
- youtrade-qqg: Phase 3 - Presentation, theme, and auth guard
- youtrade-mv8: Phase 4 - UI screens from mockups
- youtrade-4hy: Exchange Management screen
- youtrade-75g: Phase 5 - Integration, navigation, and verification

## Architecture
- Domain: entities (Ticker, Candle, OrderBook, Trade, Symbol, Venue), source contracts, MarketDataRepository interface, use cases, ExchangeCapability registry.
- Data: per-exchange REST clients, WebSocket clients, DTOs, adapters, MarketDataRepositoryImpl, Drift cache, mock data store, secure settings.
- Presentation: Riverpod providers and notifiers for tickers, candles, order books, portfolio, theme, auth guard.
- UI: screens matching mockups exactly, reusable widgets, candlestick charts (candlesticks), sparklines (fl_chart).

## Design Rationale
### Problem
The mockups describe a sophisticated multi-venue terminal but contain only synthetic client-side data. We need a real architecture that can graduate from prototype to production without rewriting everything.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- /mockups/YouTrade.dc.html:1-1113 contains the full interactive prototype with screens, theme tokens, component specs, and synthetic data model.
- /mockups/colors_and_type.css:1-301 defines OpenTreasury/YouTrade design tokens (fonts, brand colors, light/dark variables).
- /mockups/ios-frame.jsx:1-349 provides iOS 26 Liquid Glass frame primitives to mirror in Flutter.

**External:**
- CCXT has no Dart/Flutter SDK (docs.ccxt.com, github.com/ccxt/ccxt/issues/4861). Direct public REST/WebSocket calls from Flutter are the chosen path for this epic.
- Binance, Bybit, OKX, Coinbase all expose public REST + WebSocket market data without API keys.
- Recommended packages: riverpod, web_socket_channel, http, candlesticks, fl_chart, drift, local_auth, flutter_secure_storage, go_router.

### Approaches Considered
1. **Direct public REST/WebSocket from Flutter** (chosen)
   - Pros: No backend to run, faster prototype, matches user choice.
   - Cons: Each exchange has different formats; requires adapters.
   - Chosen because: aligns with hybrid prototype + real data scope.

2. **CCXT backend proxy**
   - Pros: Unified, secrets stay server-side.
   - Cons: Requires backend deployment; overkill for public-data-only first build.
   - Rejected because: out of scope for this epic.

3. **Use exchange-specific Dart packages (e.g. babel_binance)**
   - Pros: Less parsing code.
   - Cons: Limited exchange coverage, inconsistent APIs, dependency risk.
   - Rejected because: does not satisfy multi-venue requirement cleanly.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- All mockup screens and navigation with the same layout, components, and visual design.
- Domain architecture, per-exchange REST/WebSocket clients, adapters.
- Mock-first offline mode + real online public market data.
- Theme system (Flux/Carbon, dark/light), local auth gate.

**Out of scope (deferred):**
- Real trading (create/cancel orders, balances from private APIs).
- User accounts, cloud sync, push notifications.
- Real portfolio P&L from exchange positions.
- Options chain real data (will be mock/synthetic).

### Open Questions
- Which exact timeframes to support for candles? (decide during implementation)
- Should the app support horizontal orientation or stay portrait-only? (decide during implementation)
- How many candles to cache locally? (decide during implementation)

## Goal
Implement the Compare screen from the YouTrade mockups as a standalone Scaffold. Use static mock comparison data and fl_chart to render two normalized price series, symbol selector chips, a time range selector, and correlation/ratio/return/volatility stats.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

- [ ] lib/ui/widgets/compare/ contains symbol_selector.dart, compare_chart.dart, stat_card.dart, plus time_range_selector.dart if needed.
- [ ] The compare chart uses fl_chart LineChart to plot normalized percentage series for the selected symbols.
- [ ] Tapping symbol chips and time range chips updates the selected state and re-renders the chart/stats.
- [ ] test/ui/screens/compare_screen_test.dart passes, verifying the screen renders without overflow and shows CompareChart and SymbolSelector.
- [ ] flutter analyze, flutter test, and dart format --set-exit-if-changed lib test all pass.
- [ ] git commit with message \"feat(youtrade-ocj): build Compare screen from mockups\".

## Implementation Checklist
- [ ] Read /mockups/YouTrade.dc.html Compare section (lines 449-477) for visual structure.
- [ ] Create lib/ui/screens/compare_screen.dart as StatelessWidget/Scaffold using AppTheme colors and AppColorTheme.
- [ ] Create lib/ui/widgets/compare/symbol_selector.dart: horizontal scrollable chips, supports selecting/deselecting symbols, max two selected for the two-series chart.
- [ ] Create lib/ui/widgets/compare/time_range_selector.dart: horizontal chips for 1D/1W/1M/3M/1Y, updates selected range.
- [ ] Create lib/ui/widgets/compare/compare_chart.dart: fl_chart LineChart plotting normalized percentage returns, themed grid/border.
- [ ] Create lib/ui/widgets/compare/stat_card.dart and a stats table showing correlation, ratio, return, volatility for selected symbols.
- [ ] Add static mock comparison data inside compare_screen.dart or a small local model.
- [ ] Add test/ui/screens/compare_screen_test.dart using WidgetTester, MaterialApp with AppTheme.dark(AppVisualDirection.flux), checking CompareChart and SymbolSelector exist and no overflow on 390x844.
- [ ] Run flutter test, flutter analyze, dart format --set-exit-if-changed lib test; fix issues.
- [ ] Commit and close br issue.

## Key Considerations
**Mock Data Only**: No exchange API calls; all series and stats are hard-coded.
**Two-Series Limit**: Keep chart readable; selector enforces exactly two selected symbols.
**Theme Consistency**: Use colorScheme.surface, appColors.borderSubtle, appColors.accent, bullish/bearish colors.
**Overflow Safety**: Wrap selector rows in SingleChildScrollView; screen body scrolls; chart has fixed height.
**Accessibility**: Chip labels and stat labels use existing text theme; no custom colors that fail contrast.

## Anti-patterns
- No exchange API secrets or private keys stored in the Flutter app.
- No direct exchange-specific logic in UI or use cases (architecture: must go through repository/interfaces).
- No giant exchange interface that forces every venue to implement unsupported methods (SOLID: interface segregation).
- No TODO stubs for core domain entities or repository contract.
- No polling for real-time data; use WebSocket streams for active symbols.
- No ad-hoc state mutation outside notifiers.
- No deviation from the mockup design system without explicit user approval.

design:
## Goal
Build the Exchange Detail screen from the YouTrade mockups as a standalone Scaffold using the existing theme system. The screen must display venue logo/name, allocation percentage, connected/API status, balance summary, asset balances, 24h P&L, recent trades, and API key status using static mock data.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Success Criteria
- [ ] lib/ui/screens/exchange_detail_screen.dart exists and compiles as a standalone Scaffold.
- [ ] All reusable widgets live under lib/ui/widgets/exchange_detail/ and use only the existing theme/extensions and Venue enum.
- [ ] Screen renders venue name, balance, 24h P&L, API status, asset balances, and recent trades with static mock data.
- [ ] test/ui/screens/exchange_detail_screen_test.dart passes and verifies the screen renders without overflow and shows venue name, balance, and status.
- [ ] flutter analyze passes with zero warnings/errors.
- [ ] flutter test passes.
- [ ] dart format --set-exit-if-changed lib test passes.

## Implementation Checklist
- [ ] Read mockups/YouTrade.dc.html exchange-detail section to extract layout, spacing, typography, and colors.
- [ ] Create lib/ui/screens/exchange_detail_screen.dart with static mock data for Venue.binance.
- [ ] Create lib/ui/widgets/exchange_detail/venue_header.dart for venue dot, name, kinds label.
- [ ] Create lib/ui/widgets/exchange_detail/api_status_banner.dart for API LIVE indicator and API key status.
- [ ] Create lib/ui/widgets/exchange_detail/balance_card.dart and pnl_card.dart for the summary row.

- [ ] Create lib/ui/widgets/exchange_detail/trade_history_list.dart for recent trades.
- [ ] Compose screen with SafeArea, SingleChildScrollView, and horizontal scrollable venue chips.
- [ ] Add test/ui/screens/exchange_detail_screen_test.dart with MaterialApp wrapper and overflow assertion.
- [ ] Run flutter test, flutter analyze, and dart format; fix any issues.
- [ ] Commit with feat(youtrade-tj9): build Exchange Detail screen from mockups.

## Key Considerations

**Edge Case: Overflow**
- The screen must scroll vertically and the venue chip row must scroll horizontally to avoid RenderFlex overflow on small devices.
- Balance/PnL cards use Expanded inside a Row so they share available width.

**Edge Case: Theme/Direction**
- Widgets must read Theme.of(context) and AppColorTheme extension; no hard-coded colors.
- Light/dark and Flux/Carbon directions are handled by the existing theme system.

**Edge Case: Empty Lists**
- Static mock data always includes at least one asset and one trade, but widgets should not crash with empty lists.

**Edge Case: Text Fitting**
- Large balance values use FittedBox or font sizing to avoid overflow.

## Anti-patterns
- No exchange API secrets or private keys stored in the Flutter app.
- No direct exchange-specific logic in UI or use cases; business logic goes through repository/interfaces.
- No giant exchange interface forcing unsupported methods.
- No TODO stubs for core domain entities or repository contracts.
- No polling for real-time data; this screen uses static mock data only.
- No ad-hoc state mutation outside notifiers.
- No deviation from the mockup design system without explicit user approval.

## Goal
Build the Portfolio/Home screen from the YouTrade.dc.html mockup as a standalone Scaffold, matching the dark-first design system and using existing Riverpod theme providers. The screen displays aggregated net worth, an equity curve, allocation by venue, exchange cards, and open positions.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `lib/ui/screens/portfolio_screen.dart` exists and renders without overflow on a 390x844 iPhone-sized viewport.
- [ ] Screen matches mockup sections: iOS-style status bar, app header with theme/direction toggles, total equity, 24h delta, equity curve, allocation bar, exchange cards, and open positions list.
- [ ] Reusable widgets live only under `lib/ui/widgets/portfolio/` (`exchange_card.dart`, `position_tile.dart`, `equity_curve.dart`, `allocation_bar.dart`).
- [ ] Uses `flutter_riverpod` and existing theme extensions (`AppColorTheme`, `appThemeProvider`, `themeSettingsProvider`).
- [ ] `test/ui/screens/portfolio_screen_test.dart` passes and verifies: total equity shown, allocation bar present, at least one exchange card rendered, at least one position tile rendered, no overflow exceptions.
- [ ] `flutter test`, `flutter analyze`, and `dart format --set-exit-if-changed lib test` all pass.

## Implementation Checklist
- [ ] Read `mockups/YouTrade.dc.html` to extract layout, colors, typography, and spacing for the Home/Portfolio screen.
- [ ] Create reusable widgets:
  - `lib/ui/widgets/portfolio/allocation_bar.dart` — segmented horizontal bar with exchange colors and share percentages.
  - `lib/ui/widgets/portfolio/exchange_card.dart` — venue avatar, name, kinds, live dot, value, and percentage.
  - `lib/ui/widgets/portfolio/position_tile.dart` — symbol avatar, symbol/side badge, venue/qty, value, PnL.
  - `lib/ui/widgets/portfolio/equity_curve.dart` — mock equity line chart using `fl_chart` with 1D/1W/1M/1Y/ALL range chips.
- [ ] Implement `lib/ui/screens/portfolio_screen.dart`:
  - `ConsumerWidget` or `ConsumerStatefulWidget` observing `themeSettingsProvider`.
  - Static mock portfolio data (no real network calls or exchange secrets).
  - Header toggles call `ref.read(themeSettingsProvider.notifier).toggleLightDark()` and `toggleVisualDirection()`.
  - Scrollable body with all mockup sections.
  - Placeholder bottom tab bar row (Phase 5 integrates real navigation).
- [ ] Write `test/ui/screens/portfolio_screen_test.dart` using `ProviderScope` and `MaterialApp` with `appThemeProvider`.
- [ ] Run verification commands and fix analyzer/formatting issues.
- [ ] Commit with the specified message.

## Key Considerations

**Edge Case: Empty or missing providers**
- If a required provider is missing, use static mock data widgets instead of introducing new production dependencies.
- Do not create TODO stubs for domain entities or repository contracts.

**Edge Case: Theme/direction changes**
- Toggles must update `themeSettingsProvider` state; the widget must rebuild via `ConsumerWidget`/`ref.watch`.
- Test should run with default dark Flux theme to avoid golden failures.

**Edge Case: Layout overflow**
- Wrap screen body in `SingleChildScrollView`.
- Avoid unbounded vertical growth in lists by using `Column` inside scroll view (all mock items fixed).
- Test on 390x844 logical viewport to catch overflow.

**Edge Case: Accessibility**
- Use `IconButton`/`TextButton` semantics for toggles and tappable cards.
- Provide `Tooltip` for icon-only header actions.

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ No exchange API secrets or private keys stored in the Flutter app.
- ❌ No direct exchange-specific logic in UI or use cases (architecture: must go through repository/interfaces).
- ❌ No giant exchange interface that forces every venue to implement unsupported methods (SOLID: interface segregation).
- ❌ No TODO stubs for core domain entities or repository contract.
- ❌ No polling for real-time data; use WebSocket streams for active symbols (static mock data allowed here).
- ❌ No ad-hoc state mutation outside notifiers.
- ❌ No deviation from the mockup design system without explicit user approval.
- ❌ Do not modify `lib/main.dart` or global routing.
- ❌ Do not create bottom navigation here (Phase 5 handles integration).
